# Safe operations: snapshot → reset → verify snapshot exists

# Setup
exec git init
exec git config user.email test@test.com
exec git config user.name Test
exec $GOT_BIN init

# Create initial commit
exec sh -c 'echo first > file1.txt'
exec git add .
exec $GOT_BIN commit -m feat:first
stdout 'Committed'

# Create a second commit
exec sh -c 'echo second > file2.txt'
exec git add .
exec $GOT_BIN commit -m feat:second
stdout 'Committed'

# Verify we have 2 commits
exec git log --oneline
stdout feat:first
stdout feat:second

# Manual snapshot
exec $GOT_BIN snapshot create --reason before-reset
stdout 'Snapshot created'

# List snapshots
exec $GOT_BIN snapshot list
stdout 'before-reset'

# Safe reset (hard) to go back one commit
exec $GOT_BIN safe reset --mode hard HEAD~1
stdout 'Reset'
stderr 'Snapshot'

# Verify we are back to 1 commit
exec git log --oneline
stdout feat:first

# Verify snapshot is still there
exec $GOT_BIN snapshot list
stdout 'before-reset'
